title: How to fold routing targets down to the 9 workflows (#336 area 3)
context: The dependency sweep found the folded workflow names (bug-fix/refactor/migration/cleanup/investigation) are NOT truly redundant: they are wired into the 24-member CLASSIFICATION_WORKFLOWS union, a JSON-schema mirror, and 7 behavior/lane/budget/retrieval sets (router.ts, post-classifier.ts, llm-skip-evaluator.ts, retrieval-context.ts, active-implementation.ts, context-budget-estimator.ts, retrieval-depth-router.ts), plus ~15 keyed test files. 4 of the target-9 (design-test, design-retest, rules-analyze, rules-generate) are not even in the union. AC-2 only requires that code-change intents ROUTE to feature-development, not that the fine-grained tags be deleted. Your non-negotiable is 'do not break feature-development workflows and stages.'

options.label: Reroute layer: keep the union + internal behavior sets intact; add a resolveRoutedWorkflow() normalizer that collapses the fine-grained classification to exactly one of the 9 routing OUTCOMES (evidence/state/RAG/lane gating). Lowest risk, preserves lane/budget nuance, minimal test churn.

Hard-delete: remove bug-fix/refactor/migration/cleanup/investigation from the union + mirror + all 7 sets + 3 routing tables + rewrite ~15 tests. Cleaner end-state, but high risk, large coverage/CI churn, and can break feature-dev lane selection.

rationale: Dependency sweep proved the folded names carry internal lane/budget/retrieval behavior (7 sets + 15 tests); AC-2 only requires routing OUTCOME=feature-development. Reroute layer preserves internals and best honors the non-negotiable 'do not break feature-development workflows and stages'.
